Training Experience:

September, 1995 Began Tai Chi Chuan studies with Shi Fu Alex Feng

    at Wu Tao Kuan (Berkeley, CA).

August, 1996 Traveled to China with Shi Fu Feng to train at the Shanghai

    Institute of Physical Education under Professor Zeng Mei-

    Ying for two weeks. Studied the 42-step Tai Chi Sword set.

March, 1997 Began Judo studies with Sensei Alex Feng. Stopped after 6

    months due to injury and time conflicts.

April, 1997 Traveled to China to train at the Beijing Physical Education

    University under Professor Li Qiao Ling for three days.

    Received critique on 42-step Tai Chi competition set.

September, 1997 Private classes with Michelle Dwyer for one month to learn

    Yang-style two-person sparring set.

October, 1998 Private class with Professor Wang Pei Kun, Dean of Wushu

    Department, Shanghai Institute of Physical Education.

    Received critique on 42-step Tai Chi Sword competition set.

January, 1999 Began co-leading Tai Chi classes at Wu Tao Kuan and giving

    private lessons to individuals.

March, 2000 Began Chinese Acrobatic training at the Circus Center,

    (San Francisco, CA) under Xia Kemin and Weng Xiao Hong.

August, 2000 Began Wushu training in spear and straight sword with

    Teacher Tan Shenan.

June, 2001 Traveled to Shanghai with Shifu Feng to train at Chin Woo

    Association. Studied 9-sectional whip under Teacher Wang San.

August, 2002 Private lessons with Professor Wang Pei Kun covering 42-

    step barehand and sword competition forms for one week.
 
 

Tournaments:

Beginner division

November, 1996 USA Wushu Kungfu Federation (USAWKF) International

Wushu Kungfu Championships, San Francisco, CA.

Gold medals in Yang style, Yang style weapons

and 42-step.

February, 1997 Martial Arts Championships, Hayward, CA.

Gold medals in Chinese kata and weapons.

Intermediate division

March, 1997 Lone Wolf Classic, Albany, CA.

Gold medals in Chinese kata and weapons.

August, 1997 International Cup Open Martial Arts Championships,

San Mateo, CA.

Gold medals in Traditional Tai Chi, Yang style

and Tai Chi sword.

Advanced division

December, 1997 USAWKF Wushu Kungfu Festival and Championships,

Baltimore, MD.

Silver medal in Chen style

Bronze medals in 24-step Yang style, Yang style

weapons and 42-step.

April, 1998 6th Annual Western Regional Wushu Kungfu Championships,

Berkeley, CA.

Gold medal in Yang style weapons.

Bronze medals in 24-step Yang style and Chen style.

Fourth place in 42-step.

August, 1998 USAWKF International Wushu Kungfu Festival and

Championships, Baltimore, MD.

Bronze medals in 24-step Yang style, Yang style

weapons and Chen style.

Fourth place in 42-step.
 
 

April, 1999 7th Annual Western Regional Wushu Kungfu Championships,

Berkeley, CA.

Gold medal in 42-step sword.

Silver medal in 42-step.

Bronze medal in 24-step Yang style.

Fourth place in Chen style.

Bronze medal in group division.

June, 1999 Taiji Legacy & Kungfu Championships, Dallas, TX.

Gold medal in 24-step.

Bronze medals in Chen style, 42-step and Yang

style weapons.

July, 1999 A Taste of China, Winchester, VA.

Gold medal in 24-step.

Silver medal in 42-step.

April, 2001 9th Annual Western Regional Wushu Kungfu Championships,

Berkeley, CA.

Silver medal in 24-step.

Bronze medals in 42-step sword and Chen style.

Fourth place in 42-step.

November, 2001 USAWKF San Diego Nationals

Womens Internal Grand Champion Winner

Gold medals in 42-step sword and Chen style.

Silver medal in 24-step.

Bronze medal in 42-step.

April, 2002 10th Annual UC Berkeley Chinese Martial Arts Tournament

Bronze medal All-Around Internal Style

Gold medal in 24-step.

Silver medals in 42-step sword and Chen style.
 
 

Teaching Experience:

Qigong workshops at PAWMAs 25th Annual Training Camp, September 6-9, 2002
 
 

Memberships:

Pacific Association of Women Martial Artists (PAWMA)

US Wushu Union
